question: A single process instance is stuck on one service task and the operator wants execution to continue at a downstream task in that same instance. Which Operate capability is the documented fit?

options.text: Process instance batch modification, because it is the canonical way to recover a stuck instance.
Roll the instance back to the previously completed activity using Operate's history view.
Process instance modification on that single instance to move the token to the downstream task.
Redeploy the BPMN with a higher version and migrate just that instance forward.

a.text: Batch modification is for cases where many instances ended up in the wrong branch and you want to fix them together, not for a single stuck instance.

b.text: Operate cannot roll an instance back to a previously completed activity; history is read-only.

c.text: Process instance modification on the single instance is what the docs prescribe when execution is stuck and you want to skip or repeat activities for that instance.

d.text: Migration changes the process definition version of running instances; it is not how a stuck instance resumes at a different activity.

explanation: Per the Operate modification docs, you modify an active instance to allow execution to continue, for example to skip or repeat activities. For one stuck instance use process instance modification; for the same fix across many instances in the wrong branch, use process instance batch modification instead.
